text: Wireless Communications for 5G and Beyond Cellular networks and technologies are the backbone of today’s mobile communication systems. The key wireless communication technologies in the evolution of mobile communication systems are the first, second, third and fourth generations of cellular wireless networks (1G–4G). This article is a survey of the key emerging technologies that will define the fifth generation (5G) of cellular wireless networks. The key challenges that the 5G systems will encounter include providing a higher data rate, better quality of experience, and very low latency. A multi-tier network architecture based on the principle of network densification and the idea of achieving high spectral efficiency are thought to be appropriate solutions to address the key challenges of 5G systems. This article reviews several enabling technologies that address the challenges of 5G systems. Some of the key enabling technologies of 5G systems discussed in this article include massive MIMO and millimeter wave communications, full duplex wireless transmission and dense heterogeneous networks. The article also discusses some of the transformative radio access network (RN) architectures including Cloud Radio Access Networks (C-RAN) and wireless network virtualization as well as RF energy harvesting technologies for sustainable operation of 5G systems. The article also highlights some of the key research challenges in the radio resource management for 5G systems and the measurement and testing challenges associated with component-level testing and end-device testing of 5G systems. Overall, this article is intended to provide a comprehensive overview of the emerging technologies that will be used to define 5G and also provide a road map for the integration of these technologies into a single mobile standard with high performance features.
